title: daucus carota subsp. annuus
intro: Daucus carota subsp. annuus, also known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family Apiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Daucus carota subsp. annuus include reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, as well as supporting the immune system and protecting against certain cancers. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be made by infusing the flowers and roots in water or a solvent, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
